description: A tooth is one hard structure rooted in the jaw and used to bite or grind food. Context or number can also present the teeth collectively. 'tholau' (bone) covers skeletal material, while 'kulai' keeps the tooth as its own oral structure.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release into high-back rounded 'u'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l', let air pass along its sides, and open into stressed central unrounded 'a'. Raise the tongue into high-front unrounded 'i' for the separate final syllable.
usage_notes: A numeral identifies one or more teeth when the count matters. A body-part or position phrase can locate a particular tooth without requiring a separate name for every kind.

semantic_domains.physical: a hard oral structure used for biting and grinding food
